blocks.content: Savannah found him at the water fountain after fourth period with the expression she had when she'd decided to say something.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I'm going to say something and I need you to not be defensive.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Ryan asked Daisy to the winter formal.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: What do you want me to say, Savannah?

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I want you to say what you actually want to say.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She should go if she wants to go. He's a good guy.
Savannah looked at him for a long second with the particular expression of someone watching someone else miss the point on purpose.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: You know, for someone who's supposed to be her best friend—

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Savannah. I said she should go. I mean it.

She held his gaze for one more second and then walked away, and Kit stood at the water fountain and looked at nothing and said to himself, very clearly, for the first time: you are running out of time.
He didn't know what to do with that yet.
